title: Slow-cooker apple crumble
subtitle: Bramley apples under a butter and oat topping
excerpt: Bramley apples with caster sugar and cinnamon topped with a butter, oat, and brown sugar crumble, cooked on high for two hours. The filling softens and becomes saucy while the topping sets. Finish under a hot grill for three minutes to crisp the top if you want the traditional crunch.
sourceNotes: Apple crumble became Britain's favourite pudding in the postwar period as an economical alternative to pastry. Bramley apples are the standard choice because they collapse and become fluffy when cooked — eating apples hold their shape but lack the sharpness that contrasts with the sweet crumble. The slow cooker version requires the grill step to finish, but the filling is better than a rushed oven version.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the filling a day ahead. Reheat in the slow cooker, then add fresh crumble and grill.

content.text: The crumble will not crisp in the slow cooker — steam from the apple filling keeps the topping soft throughout the cook. This is fine: the topping sets and bakes, but it needs three minutes under a hot grill afterwards to go golden and crunchy. If you skip the grill step you will have a delicious soft topping, but not a crunchy crumble.

Toss the {{apple-bramley}} slices with the {{caster-sugar}} and {{cinnamon-ground}} for the filling. Spread evenly in the slow cooker. Make the crumble by rubbing the cold {{unsalted-butter}} into the {{plain-flour}} with your fingertips until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Stir in the {{rolled-oats}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, and remaining {{cinnamon-ground}}. Spread the crumble over the apple layer in an even layer — do not press it down. Cook on high for 2 hours, until the apple filling is bubbling and the crumble is set. Lift the slow cooker insert and place under a hot grill for 3 to 4 minutes until the crumble is golden and crunchy. Watch it closely: it burns fast. Serve immediately.

items.cause: Bramley apples release a lot of juice when cooked
items.fix: Toss the apple slices with the sugar and leave for 10 minutes before adding to the slow cooker — some juice will draw out, which you can drain off. Or add 1 tablespoon of plain flour to the filling toss to help thicken the juices

items.symptom: Crumble topping soggy even after grilling
items.cause: Too much steam trapped, or apples very wet
items.fix: Prop the lid slightly ajar with a wooden spoon for the last 30 minutes to let steam escape. This prevents the topping from becoming waterlogged

items.symptom: Filling not cooked through after 2 hours
items.cause: Apple slices cut too thick, or slow cooker runs cool
items.fix: Cut apple into slices no thicker than 1 cm. Continue on high for another 30 minutes. Bramley apples should collapse almost completely when properly cooked

content.text: Apple crumble is the most-made British pudding and the slow cooker version produces the best filling (soft, jammy, and saucy) with less washing up than the oven version. The grill step takes three minutes and is worth the extra pan. Serve with custard, clotted cream, or vanilla ice cream.
